title: Fixing a Memoized React Component Re-renders Due to Changing Nested Object References

statement.markdown: A memoized React component is used to render a list of items, where each item contains a complex object with nested properties. The component re-renders unnecessarily when the nested object properties change, despite the top-level object reference remaining the same. Fix the bug to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: A JSON object representing the complex data structure with nested properties
statement.outputFormat: The rendered list of items as a string
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the top-level object reference changes or when the nested object properties change in a way that affects the rendered output.

constraints.description: The number of items in the list
The number of nested properties in each item

examples.explanation: The component should re-render when the nested object properties change, but not when the top-level object reference remains the same.

hints.text: Understand the problem and the buggy behavior
Identify the logical bug in the code
Develop a solution to fix the bug

editorial.title: Fixing a Memoized React Component Re-renders Due to Changing Nested Object References
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to understand the differences between object references and object equality. The memoized component should only re-render when the top-level object reference changes or when the nested object properties change in a way that affects the rendered output.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve rewriting the entire component to use a different data structure, but this is not necessary. A more elegant solution involves using a library like `lodash` to help with object equality checks.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution involves using a library like `lodash` to help with object equality checks and a custom `useMemo` hook to memoize the component's state.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using the `useMemo` hook to memoize the component's state, and a custom `isEqual` function to check for object equality.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves demonstrating that the component only re-renders when the top-level object reference changes or when the nested object properties change in a way that affects the rendered output.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution should involve testing the component with different inputs and verifying that it only re-renders when necessary.
commonMistakes: Failing to understand the difference between object references and object equality
Not using a library like `lodash` to help with object equality checks

testCases.input: {"data": [{"id": 1, "name": "Item 1", "details": {"price": 10.99, "description": "This is item 1 changed"}}, {"id": 2, "name": "Item 2", "details": {"price": 5.99, "description": "This is item 2 changed"}}]}
testCases.output: [{"id": 1, "name": "Item 1", "details": {"price": 10.99, "description": "This is item 1 changed"}}, {"id": 2, "name": "Item 2", "details": {"price": 5.99, "description": "This is item 2 changed"}}]
